content.assessment: The demographic lens indicates this counterfactual baseline is unsustainable given the trajectory already underway. Cohort collapse (22% birth decline 2021-2025, TFR at 1.31) is generalized across Hungary but geographically uneven, with Zala County and other high-average-age municipalities facing steeper contraction than Budapest. Continuing informal, discretionary closure practice without rule-based criteria means the demographic cliff (as in Garab, a zero-enrollment case) will recur unpredictably across the one-third of settlements under 500 residents, with survival determined by social capital rather than cohort viability. Without a statutory framework, the 114 localities with under-18 populations below 10% face closure decisions disconnected from any planning horizon, and church-sector expansion (a documented sorting mechanism) proceeds unchecked as an emergent response to the same demographic pressure.
opportunities: Serves as a clear counterfactual baseline for demonstrating the marginal demographic value-added of any statutory alternative
strengths: Accurately reflects that structural fiscal/enrollment pressure from cohort decline will operate regardless of governance form, making it a realistic no-action baseline against which other proposals can be measured
threats: Continued informal reversal-by-protest dynamics could mask rather than resolve underlying cohort contraction, deferring an inevitable adjustment to a moment of greater demographic and fiscal stress
Uneven local mobilization capacity means demographically similar villages will face divergent survival outcomes, undermining any coherent national planning horizon
weaknesses: Provides no mechanism to anticipate or plan for the demographic zero-enrollment cliff pattern documented in the smallest settlements
Leaves geographically clustered aging (42 municipalities with average age above 50, concentrated in Vas and Zala) without any differentiated response
Allows demographic decline to interact unpredictably with social capital rather than being addressed via planning horizon or spatial criteria
